registry.totalBoardsNote: Appointed-positions directory · applications accepted year-round (no central vacancy list)
registry.contextNote: Texas publishes no central vacancy list — boards shown are appointment opportunities; terms are staggered six-year and applications are accepted year-round through the Governor's Appointments Office.

rows.name: Alzheimer's Disease and Related Disorders, Texas Council on
rows.constituent: Texans affected by Alzheimer's & related disorders
rows.criticalNote: 4 of 15 seats are governor-appointed
rows.mandate: Guides the state plan on Alzheimer's disease. Fifteen members — four each appointed by the Governor, Lt. Governor, and Speaker, plus three agency representatives (Health & Safety Code ch. 101).

rows.name: Behavioral Health Executive Council, Texas
rows.constituent: Texans served by licensed behavioral health professionals
rows.criticalNote: 1 of 9 seats is governor-appointed
rows.mandate: Oversees licensing and enforcement for psychologists, counselors, social workers, and marriage & family therapists. Nine members — eight appointed by member boards, one governor-appointed public member (Occ. Code 507.051).

rows.name: Diabetes Council, Texas
rows.constituent: Texans living with or at risk of diabetes
rows.criticalNote: 11 of 16 seats are governor-appointed
rows.mandate: Advises the legislature on diabetes policy and administers the state diabetes plan. Sixteen members — eleven governor-appointed citizens plus five agency representatives (Health & Safety Code 103.002).
